g.text: ② WHERE age > 20 — ROW-LEVEL filter, runs BEFORE any grouping
③ GROUP BY city — bucket the 6 surviving rows (Bob and Eve already gone)
④ HAVING COUNT(*) > 2 — GROUP-LEVEL filter, runs AFTER aggregation
WHY HAVING Cannot Replace WHERE — Counter-Example
Remove WHERE: all 8 rows enter GROUP BY — groups change
NYC: Alice + Bob + Carol — COUNT = 3 (was 2 with WHERE)
LA: Dave + Eve + Frank + Grace — COUNT = 4 (was 3 with WHERE)
HAVING COUNT > 2 would return: NYC(3) AND LA(4) — WRONG
Correct result with WHERE is only: LA(3)
WHERE filters ROWS before counting — HAVING filters COUNTS after
